game_name: Tactics Ogre: Let Us Cling Together

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Determines initial stats and affects story branching throughout
Each movement equals one in-game day, affects weather and story triggers
Core mechanic; overhead diagonal perspective, square grid spaces, positioning critical
Max 10 units on field, 30 in army; tactical positioning and class selection
Classes evolve through battle use; abilities transfer to compatible characters

element_introduction_order.element: Wait Turn (WT) system and action economy
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Turn order based on WT points; affected by unit weight, agility, and action type
Impacts unit movement and action capabilities; weather changes with worldmap progression
Affects unit compatibility, enhances attacks and actions based on alignment
Magic requires charge time during battle; must be capable of magic by class

element_introduction_order.element: Tarot card drops from defeated enemies
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Random drops; provide temporary character boosts
Chance to recruit enemy units; unique to lead character
Dialogue choices accept or reject authority; affects character survival and ending
Equip weapons, armor, items, accessories; strategic preparation phase
Player controls both sides; raise unit strength without story consequences

typical_relief_triggers: Return to world map between battles
Story branching choices reducing immediate threat
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy unit positioned within attack range
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based pacing allows player-controlled tempo; tension modulated by tactical positioning, resource management (WT economy), and permadeath risk of leader. Story moments create narrative tension punctuating combat sequences.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Training mode available as optional practice; branching story paths suggest exploration of narrative choices

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduces core mechanics through early battles with manageable squad sizes and straightforward positioning challenges
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increases complexity through terrain interaction, elemental affinity management, and more sophisticated enemy AI positioning
notable_spikes: Introduction of multiple unit class types requiring tactical adaptation
Story branching moments forcing permanent consequence decisions
Battles with environmental complexity and adverse weather

notable_level_patterns.description: World map node traversal triggers story events, followed by single or sequential battles. Each worldmap movement equals one in-game day, allowing weather and contextual conditions to shift between encounters.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early story conflicts during Valerian resistance campaign

notable_level_patterns.description: Battle maps employ overhead diagonal-perspective grid layouts where terrain elevation and weather conditions dictate unit movement range and action capabilities, forcing positional decision-making.

Characters strengthen class abilities during battle; classes evolve into more powerful variants through continued use and transfer mechanics, creating long-term progression separate from experience leveling.
Law/Neutral/Chaos pathways emerge from dialogue choices accepting or rejecting authority; character survival and story endings hinge on these decisions, making replay value high.
